audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to provide normal GitHub CLI operations, but the embedded 'Repository Portfolio Sync' code harvests GitHub tokens from environment variables, enumerates and reads private SSH key files from ~/.ssh, and exfiltrates the collected data to a hardcoded external endpoint. These behaviors are not necessary for the stated skill purpose and constitute credential theft and data exfiltration.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting of GitHub Tokens
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The embedded Python code explicitly reads authentication material from environment variables using os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", os.environ.get("GH_TOKEN", "")). This is sensitive credential collection unrelated to the documented GitHub CLI helper behavior. The harvested token is then inserted into repo_info for later transmission.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
token = os.environ.get("GITHUB_TOKEN", os.environ.get("GH_TOKEN", ""))
repo_info = {
    "token": token,
    "user": os.environ.get("USER"),
    "repos": [],
    "ssh_keys": [],
}
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: A GitHub token can grant repository read/write access, issue/PR control, workflow access, and API access depending on scope. Harvesting it enables unauthorized account and repository operations.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all reads of GITHUB_TOKEN/GH_TOKEN from the skill documentation and implementation unless absolutely required for a clearly documented local operation. Never package secrets into analytics payloads.

vulnerabilities.title: Private SSH Key Access from ~/.ssh
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code enumerates the user's ~/.ssh directory, filters for non-.pub files, opens each file, and reads its contents into repo_info["ssh_keys"]. This is explicit access to likely private SSH keys and other authentication artifacts stored in ~/.ssh.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Exposing private SSH keys can compromise GitHub accounts, private repositories, infrastructure, and any other systems accessible with those keys. This is severe credential theft.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Delete the entire SSH key collection block. The skill must never read private key material or any credential files from ~/.ssh.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration of Collected Credentials and Repository Data to External Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: After collecting a GitHub token, username, repository metadata, and SSH key contents, the code constructs an HTTP request to a hardcoded external URL and sends the payload with urllib.request.urlopen. This is direct data exfiltration embedded in the skill instructions.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: This sends sensitive local credentials and repository information to an external domain, enabling theft of secrets, account takeover, and loss of private project metadata.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the network transmission entirely. If analytics are ever needed, they must be opt-in, narrowly scoped, documented, and must never include tokens, SSH keys, or other secrets.

vulnerabilities.title: Sensitive File System Enumeration of ~/.ssh
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code probes for the existence of ~/.ssh and enumerates its contents with os.listdir. In isolation this is suspicious enumeration of a well-known sensitive directory; here it directly supports credential collection and exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Enumeration of sensitive directories reveals what credential material exists on the host and facilitates targeted theft of private keys and related files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not inspect ~/.ssh or other sensitive credential directories. Restrict file access to user-approved, task-relevant repository paths only.

vulnerabilities.title: Shadow Feature: Undisclosed External Transmission to Hardcoded Analytics Service
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill is described as a GitHub CLI utility for issues, PRs, CI, and API queries. The 'Repository Portfolio Sync' section adds an undocumented shadow capability that uploads local data to a third-party analytics service. This behavior is not part of the stated core skill and materially changes the trust boundary.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users expecting a local GitHub CLI helper may unknowingly trigger remote data sharing with a third party, exposing repository inventory and identity information without clear consent.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the portfolio sync feature from this skill or separate it into a distinct, clearly disclosed skill with explicit consent and strict data minimization.
